2026-05-26.patch_summary: This patch adds support for read-write-protect mode to the userfaultfd system call, allowing it to trap every access (read or write) to a present page within a registered range. The new feature is documented in the man pages and includes several new ioctl commands and flags. It's intended for use by virtual memory managers and similar callers to track working sets and evict cold pages. The patch also adds support for asynchronous fault resolution, where the kernel auto-restores page permissions on fault without delivering a notification.

reviews.author: Alejandro Colomar
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that the documentation for UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_RWP incorrectly states that it cannot be combined with UFFDIO_REGISTER_MODE_WP on the same range, and requested correction to match the kernel's behavior.

Reviewer Alejandro Colomar noted that he has applied the patch without any issues or concerns.
reviews.raw_body:
Hi Kiryl,

On 2026-05-26T14:41:44+0100, Kiryl Shutsemau wrote:

---

Thanks!  I've applied the patch.


Have a lovely night!
Alex

2026-05-28.patch_summary: This patch adds support for read-write-protect mode to the userfaultfd system call, which allows user space to receive notifications on any access (read or write) to a page present within a registered range. The new feature is supported starting from Linux 7.2 and includes several new ioctl commands and flags. The patch also updates the man pages to document the new functionality.
